programmes.statusNote: The current 2026-27 Programme Structure confirms 24 credit points, comprising four Core Courses (12 credit points) and four Elective Courses (12 credit points), and publishes the current course titles and credits. It does not publish a course code for any Core or Elective Course. Two shared Literature titles can be found with codes in another Programme, but that partial overlap is not evidence for the complete MACSLE code table. Keep the structure closed until the current official course catalogue or authenticated course outlines identify every code; do not infer codes from titles or publish a partial pool.

The current 2026-27 Programme page confirms a 24-credit award, seven 3-credit Core Courses, four named Elective options, and an approved 6-credit Research Project route. It describes the coursework path as seven Core Courses plus one 3-credit Elective, but the official BUS6046 Internship Programme in Personal Finance Education course outline linked from the same page states that BUS6046 is worth 6 credit points. Publishing either value would contradict another current official source. Keep the structure closed until EdUHK reconciles the Internship credit value or identifies a superseding outline; do not force BUS6046 into the 3-credit elective rule or treat its older 6-credit outline as the current award rule.

The current 2026-27 Programme and detailed-course pages confirm 24 credit points: six 3-credit Core Courses, one of two 3-credit curriculum electives, and one of the 3-credit Research Project or Internship. They publish every current title and credit value but omit the course codes. Official search results and cross-Programme pages reveal only a subset of codes, which is not a complete Programme code table. Keep the structure closed until all ten current titles are mapped by a consolidated official catalogue or current course outlines; do not infer the missing codes from numbering patterns.

The current 2026-27 Programme page confirms a 24-credit award with six 3-credit Core Courses and either two of four 3-credit Specialised Courses or a 6-credit Thesis. It publishes all eleven current titles and credit requirements but no course codes. The current Master course catalogues of both co-delivering departments, Early Childhood Education and Special Education and Counselling, do not contain a complete matching code table for these new titles; a few older courses have only similar wording and are not sufficient mapping evidence. Keep the structure closed until official current outlines identify every code and the Thesis code; do not map the new curriculum onto similarly titled legacy courses.

The current 2026-27 Programme page and official prospectus confirm a 24-credit award with three 3-credit Core Courses and either five 3-credit Elective Courses or three 3-credit Elective Courses plus a 6-credit Master's Thesis. They publish all current titles and credits but omit course codes. The older official handbook maps most titles to codes, but it has a superseded four-Core structure, a 9-credit PES6260 thesis, and no code for the new Sports Coaching and Management for Selected Populations elective. Keep the structure closed until a current official catalogue identifies every code and confirms the code for the revised 6-credit thesis; do not carry forward the obsolete 2023-24 mapping as current data.

The current 2026-27 Programme page confirms a new 24-credit award comprising four 3-credit Core Courses and four of seven 3-credit Elective Courses, and publishes every current title and credit value. It does not publish the course codes. One overlapping Python course can be found under another Programme, but that isolated match is not evidence for the complete new MSc(ADS) code table. Keep the structure closed until EdUHK publishes the current course catalogue or official outlines for all eleven courses; do not infer codes from related MIT offerings.

The current 2026-27 Programme page confirms a 24-credit award comprising four 3-credit Core Courses and four of seven 3-credit Elective Courses, and publishes every current title and credit value. The current official prospectus presents the same 12-plus-12 structure but notes that the courses offered remain subject to review. Neither source publishes the current course codes, and official searches for the named Core and Elective Courses do not locate a complete current code table or authenticated outlines. Keep the structure closed until EdUHK publishes official codes for all eleven courses; do not infer codes from similarly titled learning-sciences or educational-technology courses.

The current 2026-27 Programme page confirms an 81-credit award with 48 credit points of Professional Study, 21 credit points of Practicum, 6 credit points of Research Methods and a 6-credit Evidence-based Capstone Project. The current Department of Special Education and Counselling course catalogue maps all 23 published titles to official codes SED6010-SED6026, SED6030-SED6032 and SED6065-SED6067. However, the Programme page states only that each of the four Practicum Courses carries 3-6 credit points and does not assign the exact credits of SED6026, SED6065, SED6066 and SED6067; only their combined 21-credit requirement is published. Keep the structure closed until current official outlines identify each Practicum credit value; do not infer one of the possible 3/6 distributions from the group total.

The current 2026-27 Programme page and official prospectus confirm 24 credit points: three 3-credit Core Courses, three of nine 3-credit Elective Courses, and the 6-credit Therapeutic Play Skills practicum. They publish all current titles and credits but omit the complete code table. Current official Moodle and course-sharing pages identify only a subset, including HCS6078-HCS6080, HCS6081-HCS6083, and HCS6086; the remaining cross-faculty electives are not fully mapped by a current consolidated official source. Keep the structure closed until all thirteen course codes are verified; do not publish a partial pool or infer the missing cross-faculty codes from titles.

The current 2026-27 Programme page confirms a 24-credit award with four 3-credit Core Courses and four of eight 3-credit Elective Courses. The official 2025-26 Programme Handbook maps the four Core Courses and the original six electives to MDA6001-MDA6010, while current official Moodle records also confirm INT6139 for the new Educational Leadership in Innovation Technology elective. However, the second elective added for students admitted from 2026 onwards, Public Relations Strategies in Digital Age, has no course code on the current Programme page and no authenticated current code result. Keep the structure closed until EdUHK publishes that final code; do not expose the old six-course elective pool or publish eleven of twelve current courses as a complete structure.

The current 2026-27 Programme page and official prospectus confirm 24 credit points: four 3-credit Core Courses, two of four 3-credit Elective Courses, and two required 3-credit Project Courses. They publish all ten current titles and credit values but no course codes. The Programme Handbook download requires an authenticated EdUHK account, and public official Moodle searches do not expose a complete matching code table. Keep the structure closed until EdUHK publishes accessible current codes for all ten courses; do not infer codes from AI course titles or internal numbering patterns.

The current 2026-27 Programme page confirms 24 credit points: four 3-credit Core Courses and four of six 3-credit Elective Courses, and publishes all ten current titles. Public official EdUHK staff teaching records identify INT6140 Future of Work and Workforce Trends and INT6145 Innovation, Creativity and Forecasting Skills, but the current Programme page and public curriculum module search do not provide a complete code mapping for the other eight courses. Keep the structure closed until all ten codes are officially available; do not extrapolate the missing codes from the two confirmed INT61xx records or map similarly titled courses from other Programmes.

The current 2026-27 Programme page confirms 24 credit points: five required 3-credit Core Courses and 9 elective credits completed through two or three courses, including a 6-credit Research Project option. It publishes five Core and ten Elective titles with credit values. Several cross-Programme electives have discoverable official codes, but the five new Mindfulness and Life Education Core Courses and the 6-credit Research Project do not have a public current code table in the Programme page, curriculum module search, or official search results. Keep the structure closed until the complete code mapping is published; do not substitute the older PSY6069 Mindfulness in Education for the differently titled Mindfulness Practice course or expose only the known cross-Programme electives.

The current 2026-27 Programme page confirms 24 credit points: five required 3-credit Core Courses and three of four 3-credit Elective Courses, and publishes all nine current titles and credit values. The overlapping EMPAL curriculum confirms PUA6027 Technology and Innovation Policies: Development and Trend, but the Programme page, official leaflet and public curriculum-module search do not provide codes for the other eight ESG-specific courses. Keep the structure closed until EdUHK publishes a complete current code table; do not infer the missing codes from the single shared PUA6027 elective or publish a one-course partial pool.

The current 2026-27 Programme page confirms a two-year, 87-credit professional award comprising 22 credit points of Foundation Subjects, 42 credit points of Professional Subjects and 23 credit points of Clinical Education. It publishes all current course titles and their individual 1-8 credit values, including four Clinical Education placements totalling 23 credit points and not less than 1,000 hours, but it does not publish any course codes. Public official curriculum-module searches do not expose a complete matching code table for this new Programme. Keep the structure closed until EdUHK publishes authenticated current codes for every subject and placement; do not substitute similarly themed rehabilitation, psychology or special-education modules.

The current 2026-27 Programme page and official prospectus confirm 24 credit points: four required 3-credit Core Courses and either four 3-credit Elective Courses or two 3-credit Elective Courses plus the 6-credit Independent Research. They publish all thirteen current titles and credit values but no consolidated course code table. The overlapping Digital Governance curriculum confirms LAW6008 National Security and Informational Security, LAW6010 Cyber Criminal Law and Practice in Hong Kong, and LAW6006 Independent Research, but the other ten current National Security Law courses do not have publicly accessible official codes. Keep the structure closed until EdUHK publishes a complete current mapping; do not infer the missing LAW codes from the shared courses or numbering sequence.

The current 2026-27 Programme page confirms a 24-credit award with six 3-credit Core Courses and two of four 3-credit Elective Courses, and publishes all ten titles and credit values. Current official Moodle records confirm BUS6085 Introduction to E-commerce and Digital Retail, BUS6086 Neuromarketing, MDA6014 Marketing Analytics and Data Visualisation, and BUS6087 Frontiers in E-commerce, but do not yet provide a complete mapping for the remaining current courses. Keep the structure closed until all ten codes are officially available; do not map similarly named marketing or organisational courses from other Programmes into the missing positions.

The current 2026-27 Programme page confirms Programme codes A1M116 (full-time) and C2M045 (part-time), a 24-credit-point award, four required 3-credit Core Courses and four of seven 3-credit Courses from one Specialisation. It publishes the complete Core title pool (Theories in Intangible Cultural Heritage; Methods in Research for Cultural Heritage Education and Management; Development and Management of Cultural Industries; Study on Chinese Culture) and the complete Heritage Education and Management Specialisation title pool (Cultural Heritage Studies in China; Cultural Tourism: Planning and Management; Exploring Hong Kong's Intangible Cultural Heritage; History, Society and Culture of Lingnan; Communication and Marketing in Cultural Industries; Independent Project in Cultural Heritage Management; Internship in Cultural Heritage Management). The Xiqu Specialisation and part-time mode are not open for application in 2026-27. The page publishes no course codes for any current title. Keep the structure closed until EdUHK publishes an authenticated current code table; do not infer codes from titles, Programme codes or the closed Xiqu path.

The current 2026-27 Programme page confirms Programme code A1M135 and a 24-credit award comprising six required 3-credit Core Courses and two of five 3-credit Elective Courses, including a Capstone Project option. It publishes every current title, credit value and the extra English-language eligibility condition on two electives, but it does not publish any course codes. Public official curriculum-module searches do not expose a complete matching code table. Keep the structure closed until all eleven current course codes are officially available; do not infer codes from titles or publish the uncoded course pool.

The current 2026-27 Programme page confirms Programme code A1M119 and a 24-credit award comprising four required 3-credit Core Courses and four of seven 3-credit Elective Courses. It publishes every current title and credit value and states that the seven electives may not all be offered every year, but it does not publish course codes. Public official curriculum-module searches do not provide a complete code mapping. Keep the structure closed until all eleven codes are officially verified; do not infer codes from titles or treat the full elective pool as a guaranteed annual offering.

The current 2026-27 Programme page confirms Programme code A1M128 and a 24-credit award with four required 3-credit Core Courses. Pathway one selects four of five 3-credit electives; selected full-time students may instead complete the 6-credit Capstone Project plus two electives. The page publishes all current titles and credits. Three shared electives can be mapped elsewhere to PFS6061, PUA6027 and PFS6076, but the four new Core Courses, two other electives and Capstone Project do not have a complete public current code table. Keep the structure closed until every code is officially verified; do not publish the discoverable cross-Programme subset as a complete curriculum.

The current 2026-27 Programme page confirms Programme code A1M129 and a 24-credit award comprising four required 3-credit Core Courses and four of seven 3-credit Elective Courses, including a Capstone Project option. It publishes all eleven current titles and credit values but no course codes. Although a few titles overlap other new management Programmes, the official public curriculum-module search does not expose a complete current mapping. Keep the structure closed until all eleven codes are verified; do not transfer codes from similarly titled cross-Programme courses without an official match.

The current 2026-27 Programme page confirms Programme codes A1M132/C2M059 and a 24-credit award comprising five required 3-credit Core Courses and three of five 3-credit Elective Courses, including a Capstone Project option. It publishes every current title and credit value but no course codes, and public official searches do not expose a complete current curriculum-module mapping. Keep the structure closed until all ten course codes are officially available; do not infer a science, robotics or project code sequence from the course titles.

The current 2026-27 Programme page confirms Programme codes A1M043/C2M001 and a 24-credit award completed through eight 3-credit courses. It publishes the two-Core-plus-six-Specialist route, the alternative three-Specialist-plus-Research-Project route, and ten current Areas of Focus. Several public Area pages list current Specialist Course titles, but they omit course codes; the current Core Courses, Educational Studies and English for Academic Purposes files redirect to an authenticated EdUHK SharePoint sign-in. An official public 2024-25 course-list PDF maps the older curriculum to codes, but it is not evidence that the same Core assignments and Specialist pools apply in 2026-27. Keep the structure closed until the current authenticated files or a public 2026-27 catalogue provides the complete code table and Track ownership; do not carry forward the 2024-25 mapping as the current curriculum.

The current 2026-27 Programme page confirms Programme codes A1M087/C2M019, a 24-credit award, five required 3-credit Core Courses and three 3-credit courses from one Specialisation. It confirms that English Language Education, Learning and Teaching of Chinese Language and Literature, and Curriculum Development are the three Specialisations offered in 2026-27, while Digital Approaches for Teaching Advancement and Innovative Teaching and Learning are suspended. The public curriculum-module catalogue verifies the five Core codes TLS6036, EDA6056, SED6043, PRJ6002 and PRJ6003, but the official Course List and Course Outlines page does not expose the current Specialisation course table without the restricted site flow. Keep the structure closed until all nine current Specialisation course codes and their Track ownership are publicly verifiable; do not publish the five Core Courses as a complete Programme or reuse older Specialisation lists that include suspended paths.
